contract.name: v2/autocomplete/awarding_agency.md
contract.description: Autocomplete lookup returning awarding agency candidates with toptier and subtier metadata for a search string.

search_text.description: Text fragment matched against awarding agency names or abbreviations (case-insensitive).
search_text.constraints: Required; rejecting null, empty string, or omitted values; whitespace-only strings are allowed and yield broad matches; non-string JSON values (numbers, booleans, arrays) are coerced to their string representation before search.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to include in the response.
limit.constraints: Optional; default 10; accepts positive integers and JSON numeric values (floats are truncated toward zero); integer strings with whitespace or leading zeros are allowed; decimal strings are rejected with 400; zero returns an empty result set without error; negative numbers cause a 500 HTML error; no server-imposed maximum observed up to 1,000,000.

results.description: Autocomplete matches including agency identifiers and toptier/subtier metadata.

id.description: Internal USAspending agency identifier used by the autocomplete.

toptier_flag.description: True when the match represents a toptier agency entry.

toptier_code.description: Three-digit code assigned to the toptier agency.

abbreviation.description: Agency abbreviation; observed as uppercase strings.
Subtier abbreviation; can be null for agencies without one.

messages.description: Always present. Currently always a single element containing the deprecation warning.

messages: This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts.
This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts.

body.detail: Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text
Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer

quirks: Responses always include a deprecation warning string in the `messages` array, even when no matches are found.
`limit` accepts JSON floats (truncating toward zero) and integer-like strings, but decimal strings are rejected with 400 and negative values still crash the server with an HTML 500 error.
Whitespace-only `search_text` values are treated as valid and return a broad, alphabetized list of agencies.
Non-string `search_text` inputs are stringified server-side, so booleans or arrays succeed but usually return zero matches.
500 errors are returned as HTML rather than JSON.

probes.notes: pass - omitting limit returns 10 matches and the deprecation warning.

probes.notes: pass - higher limit yields additional matches beyond default 10.

probes.notes: fail - decimal strings for limit now confirmed to trigger a validation error rather than silent truncation.

mismatches: Docs describe `limit` as a generic number, but the API enforces positive integers: decimal strings return 400 and negatives still yield HTML 500 responses.
risks: Endpoint is deprecated and may be removed or behave differently in future releases.
Negative limit inputs crash the server with HTML error payloads; clients must validate locally.
Large unfiltered queries can return hundreds of rows and may change frequently; clients should set appropriate limits.
Clients that stringify numeric inputs should ensure they send integer values; decimal strings now fail validation even though JSON floats are silently truncated.
Because non-string `search_text` inputs are coerced to strings, unexpected payload shapes may silently succeed but return zero results.

added: Captured pass-2 probes covering null subtier abbreviations, boolean search_text coercion, and decimal-string limit validation.
Documented example for agencies without a subtier abbreviation and for the 400 response triggered by a decimal string limit.
changed: Elevated schema confidence to observed and expanded the output schema to spell out result object structure.
Refined limit parameter documentation to reflect positive-integer enforcement and updated quirks and risks accordingly.
removed: Cleared the prior gap about unknown limit ceilings after testing large values without encountering a server cap.
